text.title: Theming and Design Tokens
text.summary: CSS variable setup for light/dark mode and design token discipline — no hardcoded colors in component files.

rule.statement: All colors in component files must reference CSS variables (e.g., `text-foreground`, `bg-card`, `border-input`), never hardcoded hex or HSL values.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded colors bypass the theme system, breaking dark mode and brand customization. CSS variables propagate theme changes to all consumers automatically.

rule.statement: For Next.js apps, wrap the root layout with `next-themes` ThemeProvider and add `suppressHydrationWarning` to `<html>`.
rule.rationale: Without ThemeProvider + suppressHydrationWarning, SSR renders the light theme and the client switches to dark, causing a flash of unstyled content (FOUC) on every page load.

rule.statement: Use `cn()` (clsx + tailwind-merge) as the only className combiner. Forbid template literals for merging classes.
rule.rationale: Template literals allow conflicting utilities to coexist (`p-4 p-2` → both applied, last wins by CSS cascade). `tailwind-merge` resolves conflicts in the same utility group intelligently.
